mechanism: Bayesian game-theoretic equilibrium analysis: sellers face binary decisions on quality provision and pricing under asymmetric information; consumer payoffs depend on quality received vs. price paid; institutional restrictions (liability, verifiability) map to action space constraints; equilibrium solved via subgame perfection with mixed strategies in competition settings.

Labor market equilibrium under screening and reputation: employers choose contract design (wage, discretion, effort request); employees choose effort levels; repeated game with reputation signals updates employer beliefs about worker type; optimal contracts cluster into two regimes (control vs. trust) depending on reputation state.

Asset market price discovery under uncertainty: traders observe stochastic dividend sequence and fundamental value (FV) that declines deterministically; trading occurs in continuous double auction; equilibrium prices deviate from FV due to confusion about process; confusion measured via questionnaire and reduced by contextual reframing.

Public goods contribution game modeled as two-period game: agents decide donation (binary choice); payoffs depend on aggregate donation rate (threshold public good); allocation rule (priority for donors) creates incentives; welfare calculated as sum of health-state payoffs weighted by receipt probabilities.

Repeated public goods game with punishment: agents contribute binary choices to group account; payoff depends on aggregate contributions minus punishment costs; punishment is costly (costs punisher to reduce others' income); imperfect monitoring creates observation noise on contributions with 10% error probability.

Money-mediated trade in decentralized markets: agents randomly bilaterally match; consumer proposes terms, producer accepts/rejects; money holdings constrain purchasing power; centralized market allows rebalancing; equilibrium characterized by cash-constraint conditions and value functions solving dynamic optimization over savings decisions.
